channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Legal challenges loom as abortion is enshrined in Arizona's constitution
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona top officials certified the state’s election results Monday, including voters' approval of a measure that expands abortion access from 15 weeks to the point of fetal viability.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO AND GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to impose sweeping new tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China on first day in office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is threatening to impose sweeping new tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China as soon as he takes office as part of his efforts to crack down on illegal immigration and drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Special counsel moves to abandon election interference and classified documents cases against Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Special counsel Jack Smith moved to abandon two criminal cases against Donald Trump on Monday, acknowledging that Trump’s return to the White House will preclude attempts to federally prosecute him for retaining classified documents or trying to overturn his 2020 election defeat.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DNC union launches GoFundMe to help former staffers hit by massive layoffs after election losses
item.description: The union representing DNC staff launched a GoFundMe to raise money for those who were abruptly laid off last week – as some described the cuts as a "betrayal" of party values.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jessica Tisch to become 2nd woman to lead NYPD
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sanitation Commissioner Jessica Tisch became the top cop in New York City on Monday. She's the second woman to ever be the NYPD commissioner.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Progressives begin search for a new leader
item.description: Progressives are debating who should lead their movement as they seek to remake the Democratic Party in a new populist mold following devastating losses in November.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Brooke Rollins, Trump's pick for agriculture secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has selected former White House aide Brooke Rollins to lead the Department of Agriculture in his second administration. Here are some things to know about Trump’s choice and the agency that Rollins would lead if she is confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Rollins as agriculture chief, completing proposed slate of Cabinet secretaries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that he will nominate former White House aide Brooke Rollins to be his agriculture secretary, the last of his picks to lead executive agencies and another choice from within his established circle of advisers and allies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House mostly mum after Trump victory
item.description: The White House has taken its show largely behind closed doors after Vice President Harris’s stinging defeat to Donald Trump. Harris has all but disappeared from the public eye, jetting off to Hawaii after a few scarce appearances around Washington following her massive loss on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: How NYC congestion pricing tolls could impact drivers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul recently introduced a new plan to toll drivers who enter Manhattan below 60th Street. The revised congestion pricing plan would cost most drivers $9 during peak hours and $2.25 during off-peak hours. It's expected to go into effect in January.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to tap hedge fund manager Scott Bessent to serve as Treasury secretary in his new administration, a source confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota leads nation in voter turnout, but Wisconsin results loom
item.description: The state canvassing board met in St. Paul Thursday to certify the results of the 2024 election. Wisconsin will certify its results on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Wabasha County board tries to dock prosecutor’s pay over plea bargains
item.description: The Wabasha County Board says County Attorney Matt Stinson isn’t doing his job. And if the law would let them cut his salary, they would.
item.dc:creator: Catharine Richert and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Anti-abortion groups have a to-do list for Trump
item.description: With Trump set to begin another term and Republicans in control of Congress, the anti-abortion movement is hoping to continue the gains it made during his first time in office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
